item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Both agents consistently oppose the statement from first to final turn. Agent A argues that society matters but rights must come first because common-good claims can be abused against ordinary people. Agent B makes the same rights-boundary argument in Indonesian from a U.S. civil-liberties frame. There is no visible stance shift or flip; both concede only that society needs rules/order/responsibility under rights constraints.
